title: Neemias Queta played one of his best games as Celtics pull away late vs. 76ers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Neemias Queta led the Celtics with 27 points, 17 rebounds in a 114-98 win over the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown contributed 27 points, 8 rebounds, and 8 assists, enhancing his playmaking skills.
VJ Edgecombe made a notable effort play, saving a possession and showing sportsmanship towards a fan.

title: Parole rate is up in Mass., including for people with life sentences
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts parole rate increased by 3.5% in 2024 due to more life-sentence releases, influenced by the Mattis decision.
Life sentence parole hearings saw a rate rise from 57% in 2023 to 72% in 2024, per annual reports.
210 individuals were impacted by the Mattis decision in 2024, with 100 out of 144 eligible hearings completed.

title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A new photo of Rosalie Miller has been released to aid in the 1997 unsolved homicide case.
Miller's body was found in Auburn, New Hampshire after she disappeared in December 1996.
Officials urge those with information from the period to contact the New Hampshire Cold Case Unit.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, allegedly shot and killed Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, Rhode Island, linked to a personal dispute.
After the shooting, Sousa's vehicle crashed in Swansea, Massachusetts, killing two people; he had a firearm and ammunition.
Sousa, injured in the crash, is arrested and charged with murder; he awaits medical clearance for further custody in Cranston.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins interested in acquiring St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas before NHL trade deadline.
Thomas, 26, has consistently scored over 80 points the last two seasons, fitting Bruins' future needs.
Trade could cost Bruins significant assets, including draft picks and blue-chip prospects.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Maine approved a $3,000 settlement with no guilt admission over tree poisoning by a New York couple in Rockport.
Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett allegedly poisoned neighbor Ruth Graham's trees for an enhanced waterfront view.
The pesticide control board expressed regret for being unable to hold Antonson and Hackett accountable for the tree deaths.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots assess NFL Scouting Combine for potential draft prospects at positions of need.
Wide receivers and edge rushers highlighted as potential Patriots targets in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Prospects KC Concepcion and Romello Height headline as strong candidates following Combine performances.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are unlikely to trade major draft picks for star players like Micah Parsons, per ESPN's Mike Reiss.
They have shown interest in Eagles’ A.J. Brown and Raiders’ Maxx Crosby, but asking prices are high.
Patriots have 11 draft picks in 2026, mostly in later rounds, and $40 million in cap space.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power receives state and city approval to construct Massachusetts' largest battery energy storage system in Everett.
The facility will use lithium-ion batteries, feature 816 enclosures, and include two substations and transmission lines.
The storage system is part of the Docklands project, which will include residential, office, and retail space development.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Actor Connor Storrie hosted the February 28 episode of 'Saturday Night Live' with musical guest Mumford & Sons.
Storrie's 'Heated Rivalry' co-star, Hudson Williams, appeared in a skating sketch and helped introduce the musical guest.
Members of the US Olympic hockey teams joined the show, highlighting recent accomplishments and featuring jokes about current events.
